title: God’s Love for Man
quote: “God so loved the world, that He gave His only-begotten Son.” He gave Him not only to live among men, to bear their sins, and die their sacrifice. He gave Him to the fallen race. Christ was to identify Himself with the interests and needs of humanity. He who was one with God has linked Himself with the children of men by ties that are never to be broken. — SC 14.1
summary:
📝 **Summary**

**🌿 Nature Speaks of God’s Love**
From sunshine and rain to hills and seas, creation testifies of the Creator’s tender care. Every flower, bird, and tree speaks of His fatherly love. Even the thorns and thistles—the trials of life—are given for our good, to train us for heaven.

**📖 God’s Character Revealed**
God’s glory is His goodness, mercy, and long‑suffering. When Moses asked to see His glory, God proclaimed His compassionate nature. Yet Satan had darkened human minds, making people see God as harsh and unforgiving. Jesus came to remove this dark shadow and reveal the Father’s true character.

**💞 Jesus: The Perfect Revelation**
No one has seen the Father except the Son. In Christ’s life—healing the sick, comforting the sorrowful, showing tenderness even to children—we see the Father’s heart. Jesus never suppressed truth, but always spoke it in love.

**✝️ The Cost of Redemption**
To save us, Jesus left the glory of heaven, became a Man of Sorrows, and suffered the agony of the cross. The sense of sin’s separation from God broke His heart. But this sacrifice was not to make God love us—He already loved us, and therefore He provided the sacrifice.

**👑 Our Worth in God’s Eyes**
“Behold, what manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called the sons of God.” Through Christ, fallen humans may become children of the heavenly King. The more we study God’s character in the light of the cross, the more we see mercy, tenderness, and forgiveness flowing from His heart.

title: The Sinner’s Need of Christ
quote: “Could he be permitted to enter heaven, it would have no joy for him. The spirit of unselfish love that reigns there—every heart responding to the heart of Infinite Love—would touch no answering chord in his soul. … He would be a discordant note in the melody of heaven.” — Steps to Christ, p. 17
summary:
📝 **Summary**

**✨ Man’s Original Design**
🧠 Noble powers, balanced mind, perfect being • 💖 Pure thoughts, holy aims, joyful communion with God • 🤝 In harmony with the Creator

**💔 The Fall & Its Result**
🐍 Disobedience → powers perverted • 💢 Selfishness replaced love • ⚖️ Nature weakened → unable to resist evil • 🔗 Captive to Satan, destined for ruin without God’s intervention

**🌍 Heaven & the Unrenewed Heart**
🚫 Sinner cannot find joy in holiness • 🌑 Unrenewed heart not in harmony with God • 🏰 Heaven would be torture, not bliss, to the unchanged soul • 🔥 They are shut out by their own unfitness, not by arbitrary decree

**🛠️ Why Human Effort Alone Fails**
🧼 “Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean?” • 📚 Education, culture, willpower → can polish outside, but cannot change the heart • 💡 A power working from within is needed – a new life from above • ✝️ That power is Christ; His grace alone quickens the soul

**🌟 The Only Solution: A New Birth**
🍃 “Except a man be born from above…” (John 3:3) • 🚫 Fatal deception: thinking we just need to “develop the good within” • 💔 Paul’s cry: “O wretched man that I am! who shall deliver me?” • 🐑 One answer: “Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.”

**🪜 The Ladder – Christ Our Bridge**
🛌 Jacob’s dream: ladder from earth to heaven, angels ascending/descending • 🔗 That ladder = Jesus – the only medium of communication between God and man • 🌉 Through Christ, earth is linked again with heaven • 💪 He connects our weakness with infinite power

**❤️ God’s Unfailing Love**
🎁 In giving His Son, God poured out all heaven • 🕊️ The Father, Son, Spirit, angels – all enlisted for our redemption • 💰 Motives stronger and agencies more powerful could not be brought into play • 🏆 Eternal rewards: heaven, angelic society, communion with God, endless growth

**⚠️ A Sober Warning**
⚖️ God’s judgments against sin are real • 🔥 Inevitable retribution & final destruction – presented to warn us away from serving Satan

**✅ Practical Takeaway**
🙌 Let’s respond to His amazing love • 🔄 Place ourselves in right relation to Him • 🛠️ Use the means He provides to be transformed into His likeness • 🤝 Restored to fellowship with angels, and harmony with the Father and Son

*“What more could He do?” Let’s walk in that grace today. 🙏*

title: Repentance
quote: “Repentance includes sorrow for sin and a turning away from it. We shall not renounce sin unless we see its sinfulness; until we turn away from it in heart, there will be no real change in the life.” — Steps to Christ, p. 23.2
summary:
✨ The Nature of True Repentance

• Repentance is more than sorrow for consequences—it is sorrow for sin itself.
• True repentance turns away from sin, a change of heart that leads to a transformed life.
• ❌ False repentance: Esau, Balaam, Judas, Pharaoh—they regretted the results of sin, not the sin.

---

💧 How Repentance Comes

• Repentance is not self‑produced; it is a gift from Christ (Acts 5:31).
• The Spirit awakens the conscience, revealing God’s holiness and our sinfulness.
• As we behold Christ on the cross, His love draws us → genuine repentance.

---

⏳ Do Not Wait to Make Yourself Better

• Many think: “I must repent before coming to Christ.” But the Bible says Christ gives repentance.
• The same Spirit that convicts also draws—if we do not resist, we will be drawn to the Saviour.
• We cannot change our own hearts any more than the leopard can change its spots (Jeremiah 13:23).
• Come to Christ just as you are—He accepts the sinner who feels his need.

---

⚠️ The Danger of Procrastination

• Delaying to yield to God’s Spirit hardens the heart.
• What we do not overcome will overcome us.
• Even “small” sins (like Adam’s eating the forbidden fruit) have far‑reaching consequences; Calvary testifies to the terrible enormity of sin.

---

🕊️ The Sinner’s Only Hope

• A mere intellectual religion or outward form of godliness is not enough—the heart must be cleansed.
• The same God who convicts also invites: “Search me, O God, and know my heart” (Psalm 139:23).
• When Satan points to your sin, look to Christ and His merits. His sacrifice is sufficient for the worst of sinners.
• Those who are forgiven much love much, and they stand nearest to His throne.

---

🌟 Final Encouragement

• Do not wait for a better opportunity or a holier temper. Now is the accepted time.
• God’s tender love pleads with you; His promises and warnings are the breathing of unutterable love.
• Come to Him with your soul stained as it is. He is ready to set you free.

“Today if ye will hear His voice, harden not your hearts.” — Hebrews 3:7–8

title: Confession
quote: “He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy.” — Proverbs 28:13
summary:
💎 THE SIMPLE CONDITIONS OF MERCY

• God’s requirements for forgiveness are just, reasonable, and simple.
• We do not need to perform painful penances or long pilgrimages to expiate our sins.
• Mercy is promised to those who do two things: CONFESS and FORSAKE their transgressions.

---

🤝 CONFESSING TO GOD AND TO MAN

• TO GOD: Confess your sins to Him alone, for only He can forgive them.
• TO MAN: If you have offended a neighbor or friend, acknowledge your wrong to them.
• THE MEDIATOR: Christ, our great High Priest, is touched with the feeling of our infirmities and is able to cleanse us from every stain of iniquity (Hebrews 4:15).

---

❤️ THE CHARACTER OF TRUE CONFESSION

• It must be HEARTFELT and FREELY EXPRESSED, not forced or urged from the sinner.
• ❌ Avoid flippant or careless acknowledgments; God values the "broken heart" and "contrite spirit."
• True confession is specific—acknowledging the very sins of which you are guilty rather than making general, vague statements.

---

🛠️ CONFESSION MUST LEAD TO REFORMATION

• Confession is not acceptable without SINCERE REPENTANCE AND A CHANGE IN LIFE.
• Everything offensive to God must be put away. As the prophet said: "Cease to do evil; learn to do well" (Isaiah 1:16, 17).
• Restitution is part of the process: restoring what was robbed and walking in the statutes of life.

---

🚫 THE TRAP OF SELF-JUSTIFICATION

• When the moral perceptions are deadened, the sinner makes excuses and apologies for their course.
• ADAM & EVE: Both attempted to shift the blame—Adam to the woman (and God), and Eve to the serpent.
• True repentance bears its own guilt without hypocrisy. Like the publican, the penitent soul cries: "God be merciful to me a sinner."

---

🚩 FINAL ENCOURAGEMENT

• Do not shield your sins or paint them in light hues; follow Paul’s example of transparently acknowledging past wrongs.
• The broken heart will appreciate the love of God and the immense cost of Calvary.
• THE PROMISE: "If we confess our sins, He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness" (1 John 1:9).

title: Consecration
quote: “It is a mistake to entertain the thought that God is pleased to see His children suffer. All heaven is interested in the happiness of man. Our heavenly Father does not close the avenues of joy to any of His creatures. The divine requirements call upon us to shun those indulgences that would bring suffering and disappointment, that would close to us the door of happiness and heaven. The world's Redeemer accepts men as they are, with all their wants, imperfections, and weaknesses; and He will not only cleanse from sin and grant redemption through His blood, but will satisfy the heart-longing of all who consent to wear His yoke, to bear His burden. It is His purpose to impart peace and rest to all who come to Him for the bread of life. He requires us to perform only those duties that will lead our steps to heights of bliss to which the disobedient can never attain. The true, joyous life of the soul is to have Christ formed within, the hope of glory.” — SC 46.3
summary:
💎 THE WHOLE HEART MUST BE YIELDED

• God’s promise is conditional: we find Him when we search with ALL our heart.
• By nature we are alienated from God — “dead in trespasses and sins.”
• God desires to heal and set us free, but this requires an entire transformation, a renewing of our whole nature.

---

⚔️ THE GREATEST BATTLE

• The warfare against self is the greatest battle ever fought.
• Yielding self, surrendering all to God’s will, requires a struggle.
• But the soul must submit to God before it can be renewed in holiness.

---

🧠 GOD DOES NOT FORCE THE WILL

• God’s government appeals to intellect and conscience. “Come now, and let us reason together” (Isaiah 1:18).
• God does not accept homage that is not willingly and intelligently given.
• Forced submission would make man a mere automaton, preventing real development.
• God desires that we reach the highest possible development through His grace.

---

🚫 GIVING UP ALL

• To give ourselves to God, we must give up whatever separates us from Him.
• “Whosoever he be of you that forsaketh not all that he hath, he cannot be My disciple” (Luke 14:33).
• Mammon (love of money), reputation, worldly honor, selfish ease — these idols must be broken.
• We cannot be half the Lord’s and half the world’s.

---

❤️ LOVE, NOT MERE DUTY

• Relying on our own efforts to obey the law and earn salvation is worthless religion.
• When Christ dwells in the heart, love to Him becomes the spring of action.
• Those who feel God’s constraining love do not ask for the lowest standard — they aim at perfect conformity to His will.

---

💸 WHAT DO WE REALLY GIVE UP?

• What do we give up when we yield all? A sin-polluted heart, for Jesus to purify.
• God does not ask us to give up anything that is truly good for us.
• The path of transgression is the path of misery; true joy is found in following God’s will.

---

🤔 “HOW DO I SURRENDER?”

• You are weak in moral power, in slavery to doubt and sin. Your promises are like ropes of sand.
• But you need not despair. Understand the true force of the WILL.
• The will is the governing power — the power of decision and choice.
• You cannot change your heart, but you can CHOOSE to serve God.
• Give Him your will; He will then work in you to will and to do according to His good pleasure.

---

✅ THE DECISION THAT MATTERS

• Desires for goodness are right, but if you stop there, they avail nothing.
• Many will be lost while hoping and desiring to be Christians — they do not come to the point of yielding the will.
• Through the right exercise of the will, an entire change may be made in your life.
• By yielding your will to Christ, you ally yourself with the power above all principalities and powers.

---

🕊️ FINAL PROMISE

• Through constant surrender to God, you will be enabled to live the new life — the life of faith.
• Let your prayer be: “Take me, O Lord, as wholly Thine. I lay all my plans at Thy feet.”

title: Faith and Acceptance
quote: “It is peace that you need—Heaven's forgiveness and peace and love in the soul. Money cannot buy it, intellect cannot procure it, wisdom cannot attain to it; you can never hope, by your own efforts, to secure it. But God offers it to you as a gift, ‘without money and without price.’” — SC 49.2
summary:
💎 THE SINNER’S HELPLESSNESS

• The Spirit has shown you the evil, power, and guilt of sin. You long to be forgiven, cleansed, set free.
• Harmony with God—what can you do to obtain it? Nothing by your own efforts.
• But God offers it as a gift: “without money and without price” (Isaiah 55:1).

---

✨ BELIEVE AND RECEIVE

• “Though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow” (Isaiah 1:18).
• “A new heart also will I give you” (Ezekiel 36:26).
• You have confessed, put away sin, resolved to give yourself to God. Now ask Him to wash away your sins and give you a new heart—and BELIEVE that He does this because He promised.

---

🛌 THE LESSON FROM THE PARALYTIC

• Jesus healed the sick to inspire faith in His power to forgive sins.
• The paralytic at Bethesda had been helpless for 38 years. Jesus said, “Rise, take up thy bed, and walk.”
• The man BELIEVED the word, WILLED to walk, and God gave the power. He acted on Christ’s word and was made whole.

---

🙌 ACT ON THE PROMISE

• In like manner, you are a sinner. You cannot atone for the past or change your own heart.
• But God promises to do it through Christ. Confess, give yourself to God, and WILL to serve Him.
• As surely as you do this, God fulfills His word. If you believe you are forgiven and cleansed, God supplies the fact.

---

⏳ DO NOT WAIT TO FEEL

• Do not wait to FEEL that you are made whole. Say, “I believe it; it IS so, not because I feel it, but because God has promised.”
• “What things soever ye desire, when ye pray, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them” (Mark 11:24).
• Ask according to God’s will—and it is His will to cleanse you, make you His child, and enable you to live a holy life.

---

🕊️ NOW YOU ARE CHRIST’S

• Henceforth you are not your own; you are bought with the precious blood of Christ (1 Peter 1:18–19).
• Through this simple act of believing, the Spirit has begotten new life in you. You are a child of God.
• Do not draw back. Day by day say, “I am Christ’s; I have given myself to Him.”

---

🏃 COME AS YOU ARE

• Some think they must prove they are reformed before claiming God’s blessing. But we may claim it NOW.
• Jesus loves to have us come just as we are—sinful, helpless, dependent.
• It is His glory to encircle us in His arms, bind up our wounds, and cleanse us from all impurity.

---

❤️ THE FATHER’S HEART

• Thousands fail because they do not believe that Jesus pardons them personally.
• Put away suspicion. God’s promises are for every repentant transgressor.
• God does not deal with us as finite men. His thoughts are mercy, love, and tenderest compassion.

---

👨‍👦 THE PRODIGAL’S WELCOME

• The parable of the prodigal son shows how the wanderer is received: while he was yet a great way off, the father ran, fell on his neck, and kissed him.
• Yet even this falls short of God’s compassion. “I have loved thee with an everlasting love” (Jeremiah 31:3).

---

🌟 DO NOT DOUBT

• Can you give place to doubt when the Bible is full of such promises?
• God hates sin but loves the sinner. He gave Himself in Christ that all might be saved.
• “Can a woman forget her sucking child? … yet will I not forget thee” (Isaiah 49:15).

---

🕯️ FINAL INVITATION

• Look up, doubting soul; Jesus lives to make intercession for us.
• The Spirit invites you today. Come with your whole heart to Jesus and claim His blessing.
• “We have redemption through His blood, the forgiveness of sins” (Ephesians 1:7).
• As you draw near with confession and repentance, He will draw near with mercy and forgiveness.

title: The Test of Discipleship
quote: “If any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new.” — 2 Corinthians 5:17
summary:
🌬️ THE NEW BIRTH – INVISIBLE YET REAL

• Like the wind, the Spirit’s work cannot be seen, but its effects are unmistakable.
• Conversion may not have a dramatic moment, but a new life in Christ will bear witness.
• The character is revealed not by occasional deeds, but by the habitual direction of words and acts.

---

❤️ WHO HAS THE HEART?

• Outward correctness can exist without Christ; self‑respect or desire for esteem may produce a well‑ordered life.
• The true test: with whom are our thoughts? Of whom do we love to converse?
• If we are Christ’s, our sweetest thoughts are of Him, and all we have is consecrated to Him.

---

🍎 FRUITS OF THE SPIRIT

• Those who are new creatures bring forth love, joy, peace, long‑suffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, temperance (Galatians 5:22–23).
• The proud become meek; the vain become humble; the profligate become pure.
• Christians lay aside vain customs and adorn themselves with a meek and quiet spirit (1 Peter 3:3–4).

---

🔄 EVIDENCE OF GENUINE REPENTANCE

• True repentance works reformation: restoring what was taken, confessing sins, loving God and fellow men.
• When love springs up, duty becomes a delight, and sacrifice a pleasure.

---

⚖️ TWO DANGEROUS ERRORS

1. Trusting in works – Trying to become holy by keeping the law is impossible; all we do without Christ is polluted.
2. Believing faith releases from obedience – Faith does not abolish the law; it enables us to keep it from the heart.

• Obedience is not mere outward compliance but the service of love.
• When the law is written in the heart, it shapes the life.

---

✝️ RIGHTEOUSNESS THROUGH CHRIST ALONE

• We have no righteousness of our own; Christ offers to take our sins and give us His righteousness.
• For His sake we are accounted righteous, accepted as if we had not sinned.
• More than that, Christ changes the heart and works in us to will and to do.

---

🤲 FAITH THAT WORKS

• Faith is more than belief that devils have (James 2:19). True faith submits the will to God, fixes the affections on Him, and purifies the soul.
• Such faith delights in God’s law and fulfills its righteousness.

---

💔 ENCOURAGEMENT FOR THE DISCOURAGED

• Even when we fail, we are not cast off. Christ intercedes for us (1 John 2:1).
• The Father Himself loves you (John 16:27).
• The closer we come to Jesus, the more we see our own faults—this is evidence that the Spirit is at work.
• Our sense of need drives us to Him, and He reveals Himself in power.

---

🌟 FINAL PROMISE

• He who began a good work will carry it forward to the day of Jesus Christ.
• Trust not in feelings but in His faithfulness. Let your prayer be: “Search me, O God, and know my heart.”
